name: Hobgoblin
sourceBook: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
summary: Hobgoblins trace their origins to the ancient courts of the Feywild, where they first appeared with their goblin and bugbear kin. Many of them were driven from the Feywild by the conquering god Maglubiyet, who marshaled them as soldiers, but the fey realm left its mark; wherever they are in the multiverse, they continue to channel an aspect of the Feywild’s rule of reciprocity, which creates a mystical bond between the giver and the receiver of a gift.

stats.label: Ability Score Increase
stats.value: When determining your character’s ability scores, increase one score by 2 and increase a different score by 1, or increase three different scores by 1. You can't raise any of your scores above 20.

stats.label: Languages
stats.value: Your character can speak, read, and write Common and one other language that you and your DM agree is appropriate for the character. The Player’s Handbook offers a list of languages to choose from. The DM is free to modify that list for a campaign.

coreTraits.name: Creature Type
coreTraits.summary: You are a Humanoid. You are also considered a goblinoid for any prerequisite or effect that requires you to be a goblinoid.

coreTraits.name: Darkvision
coreTraits.summary: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

coreTraits.name: Fey Ancestry
coreTraits.summary: You have advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the charmed condition on yourself.

coreTraits.name: Hospitality
coreTraits.summary: You and the creature you help each gain a number of temporary hit points equal to 1d6 plus your proficiency bonus.

coreTraits.name: Passage
coreTraits.summary: You and the creature you help each increase your walking speeds by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.

coreTraits.name: Spite
coreTraits.summary: Until the start of your next turn, the first time the creature you help hits a target with an attack roll, that target has disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes within the next minute.

coreTraits.name: Fortune from the Many
coreTraits.summary: If you miss with an attack roll or fail an ability check or a saving throw, you can draw on your bonds of reciprocity to gain a bonus to the roll equal to the number of allies you can see within 30 feet of you (maximum bonus of +3). You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

notes: source isolated from page-content and table of contents
raw text is preserved in page.html for manual inspection
